question.text: Rohit is evaluating two Category III AIFs for an HNI client with INR 5 Crore to invest. Fund A charges 2% management fee and 20% carry above 10% hurdle. Fund B charges 1% management fee and 15% carry above 8% hurdle. Which analysis is MOST important?

answer.text: Simply choosing the fund with lower fees
Choosing based on which fund manager is more famous
Evaluating net-of-fee returns relative to risk taken and strategy fit with the investor's profile
Selecting the fund with the higher hurdle rate only

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Fee comparison alone is insufficient. Net-of-fee returns relative to risk and strategy suitability provide a holistic view. A higher-fee fund may deliver better net returns if the strategy and execution are superior. **The Trap:** Simply choosing lower fees ignores the value delivered. Fame doesn't indicate quality. Higher hurdle alone doesn't ensure better investor outcomes if performance is poor. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A compares expense ratios across mutual funds; AIF fee analysis is more complex due to performance fees and waterfall structures. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Create a fee impact analysis for clients showing projected net returns under different scenarios — this demonstrates professionalism and helps investors make informed decisions.
